title: Cortexel skill contract source (meta-schema)
description:
Every file in contract/skills/ must satisfy this. It is what makes 'one authority' enforceable rather than aspirational: a skill's scientific meaning, request schema, validators, budgets, disclosures, accessibility, evidence, ownership, and migration all live in ONE reviewable document, and every generated artifact is derived from it.

Free-form executable expressions are forbidden anywhere in a contract source. A semantic rule is a NAMED validator id with parameters — never a string of code to evaluate.

outputAuthority.description: Finite, nonrecursive OutputAuthority / AuthorityAlgebra V1. The registered evaluator accepts only a validated canonical request and independently derives expected carriers. Compiler tables, RenderPlans, artifacts, receipts and self-reported counts are not evaluator inputs.

requestPaths.description: Finite source-owned request paths. A witness refers to a registered path id; there are no wildcards, queries, JSON Pointer strings, ancestor operators or executable selectors.

derivationFields.description: The evaluator's independently typed, skill-local output vocabulary. Unknown fields fail closed.

table.description: Every cell and duplicate multiplicity must equal the evaluator-derived expected row sequence. rowsTotal is derived from those verified rows, never copied from a compiler receipt.

payloadAssurance.description: carrier_only establishes cardinality/order/provenance only. canonical_geometry_exact additionally requires the evaluator to derive the complete canonical numeric geometry payload independently.

items.description: An existential regression witness over two separately validated requests, plus a protected-output frame law. It is not a universal influence proof.

summary.description: Skill-specific value-filled SummaryFacts. The fact keys must exactly equal the template placeholders; generic ellipsis fallback is forbidden.

disclosures.description: Machine disclosure facts independently re-derived from the canonical request. The gate applies the closed registry and forced-skill rules; evaluator/compiler text can never supply disclosure wording.

id.description: The catalog id. Simulator-neutral: it names what the figure MEANS, not which simulator produced the data or which technology draws it.

revision.description: Bumped whenever this contract's accepted meaning or output changes.

status.description: Closed. There is no `beta`, no `preview`, and no free text: a status is a promise, and an ambiguous promise is a broken one.

releaseReady.description: Whether this contract's evidence is actually complete. Kept SEPARATE from `status` so a label can never substitute for evidence.

canonicalQuestion.description: The exact scientific question this figure can answer.

cannotEstablish.description: What it CANNOT establish. Required, and required to be non-empty: a contract that lists no limits is not a scientific contract.

science.description: The reviewable scientific specification. Prose here is normative: it is what an independent reviewer reads BEFORE looking at any implementation.

normalization.description: Accepted order, stable sorting, duplicate policy, missing values, signed zero, unit conversion, interval convention, category ordering.

derivation.description: Each algorithm, its formula, denominators, bins, edge handling, and complexity.

uncertaintySupport.description: Exactly which UncertaintyV1 variants this figure can render. Anything else is refused rather than approximated.

requestSchema.description: The Draft 2020-12 schemas for `data` and `parameters`. Both MUST set additionalProperties:false — a mistyped scientific field has to fail closed, not be silently ignored. Optional envelopeConstraints is a root-level Draft 2020-12 refinement for a scientific relation that crosses the otherwise separate data and parameters schemas.

envelopeConstraints.description: A root-level Draft 2020-12 schema fragment composed with the generated closed request envelope through allOf. It may refine relations across data and parameters but must not replace or reopen either closed authoring schema.

semanticValidators.description: Named cross-field rules from contract/registries/semantic-validators.v1.json. JSON Schema checks SHAPE; these check MEANING.

axisOrder.description: A fixed renderer-owned matrix orientation. This field is absent for non-matrix renderers and cannot be supplied or changed by a caller.

renderer.description: The render-plan compiler that owns this figure end to end. A stable skill MUST have one: `scene: null` is not a renderer, it is an absence of one.

maxReturnedTableRows.description: Maximum exact-value rows returned on FigureResult.table. Artifact 1.0 has no excerpt or sidecar state, so a larger table is refused.

compactionPolicies.description: Which compaction policies are scientifically VALID for this figure. An empty list means the figure refuses to compact — for a distribution, extrema sampling would destroy the mass, so refusing is the only honest option.

tablePolicy.description: V1 returns every accepted exact-value row on FigureResult.table. It has no excerpt, sidecar, or reference-only table state; an over-budget table is refused. Returned rows are not embedded in artifact JSON, whose table binding is shape-only.

requiredSourceFields.description: Source-declaration fields this figure cannot be interpreted without.

disclosures.description: Disclosure rule ids this figure may emit. Each is triggered by a machine-checkable artifact fact, never by caller text.

summaryTemplate.description: A deterministic template. It states the quantity, unit, domain, counts, method, and limitations. It contains no interpretive claim such as `significant`, `increased`, or `caused`, and it is never generated by a language model at render time.

cellType.description: Closed scalar domain for every non-null returned cell in this column. Structured values are RFC 8785 canonical JSON encoded as strings.

nullable.description: Whether a returned row may carry null in this column for any accepted request mode. Undefined and non-finite numbers are never legal cells.

keyPart.description: True when this column participates in the table's ordered composite row identity. The tuple of all keyPart columns must be unique over every returned row.

tableColumns.description: The exact ordered closed schema of the returned exact-value alternative. Required for every stable figure: a chart nobody can read the numbers out of is not accessible. Every stable table declares at least one keyPart column, and the compiler boundary/test matrix enforces the composite-key and cell-domain laws.

handVectors.description: Whether hand-computable microfixtures exist. MANDATORY for every stable formula — two libraries can share a convention error, but arithmetic you can do on paper cannot.

legacyIds.description: Pre-1.0 ids that migrate HERE. These are accepted only by explicit migration operations (the API or offline `cortexel migrate` CLI command), never by normal validation: a silent alias makes a stored artifact ambiguous about what was actually validated.

owner.description: An accountable person. A team name with no person behind it is not ownership.

changePolicy.description: Which fields, defaults, algorithms, or output facts would make a change MAJOR.

valid.description: Living fixtures. The test suite asserts these validate, so they cannot rot.

invalid.description: The NEAREST misuse for each rule, with the exact code it must produce. A rule with no invalid vector is not actually enforced.
